title: Lumen Session — 2026-03-12 (v0.1 Build Complete)
content:
# Lumen Session — 2026-03-12

## Steps Completed

### Step 0 — Architecture Decisions
- ADR-009: v0.1 code strategy — restructure Python pipeline, rewrite Electron Hub from scratch, carry forward signaling server and player client
- ADR-010: Foundry module language — JavaScript (ESM) + JSDoc annotations, no TypeScript build step
- ADR-011: Hub UI framework — Svelte 5 with Vite bundler

### Step 1 — Project Restructure
- Scaffolded `src/` directory: pipeline, hub, signaling, player-client, foundry-module
- Cleaned up Python pipeline modules from PoC into `src/pipeline/`:
  - `config.py`: default model changed to `large-v3`, RMS gate made configurable in VADConfig
  - `vad.py`: extracted `_reset_buffers()` helper, eliminated duplicated state-reset code
  - `transcription_server.py`: replaced `print()` with `logging`, added `pending_tasks` set for task tracking
- Scaffolded Electron + Svelte Hub shell with dark theme and 5-panel layout skeleton
- Scaffolded Foundry v13 module with `module.json` manifest and `lumen.mjs` stub
- Validated pipeline imports from new `src/pipeline/` structure — config, VAD, Whisper all load correctly

### Step 2 — Foundry VTT Module
- Built `src/foundry-module/scripts/lumen.mjs` (~420 lines):
  - One-time pairing flow: PAIR_REQUEST → PAIR_CHALLENGE → code displayed via `ui.notifications.info()` → PAIR_CONFIRM → PAIR_SUCCESS → token stored in world settings
  - Reconnect flow: HELLO with stored token → HELLO_ACK → skip pairing
  - HELLO_REJECT handling: clears token, re-runs full pairing flow
  - Full game state SNAPSHOT sent after authentication (scene, combat, party, players)
  - 9 Foundry hooks for delta streaming: canvasReady, updateScene, combatStart, deleteCombat, updateCombat, createCombatant, deleteCombatant, updateActor, userConnected
  - Null-safe throughout: handles missing combat, scene, HP, conditions
  - Exponential backoff reconnection: 1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 16s → cap 30s
  - PING/PONG heartbeat every 15s with 5s timeout
  - GM-only: module inactive for non-GM users
- Built `src/foundry-module/dev/mock-hub.mjs` (~130 lines) for protocol testing
- Created junction from `C:\Users\colek\AppData\Local\FoundryVTT\Data\modules\lumen-hub` to `C:\Dev\Lumen\src\foundry-module` for live development
- Validated in Foundry v13 with PF2e system: pairing flow, token persistence, HELLO on reload, 9 hooks registered

### Step 3a — Hub WebSocket Server
- Built `src/hub/main/foundry-server.js` — Hub-side implementation of the Foundry connector protocol
- Full pairing protocol, token persistence via `lumen-tokens.json` in Electron userData, HELLO/HELLO_ACK flow, SNAPSHOT/DELTA handling with IPC relay to renderer, PING/PONG heartbeat, graceful reconnection
- Wired into Electron main process via `main.js`
- 13 integration tests — all passed
- IPC bridge (`preload.js`) exposes 6 events + `getFoundryState()` to renderer

### Step 3b — Hub Transcript Viewer UI
- Built 5 Svelte components under `src/hub/renderer/components/`:
  - `StatusBar.svelte` — connection status, green/red dot, scene name in header
  - `TranscriptPanel.svelte` — scrolling transcript, speaker colors, timestamps, auto-scroll with scroll-lock, "New messages" button
  - `PlayersPanel.svelte` — party roster, HP with color coding (green/yellow/red), conditions, compact mode below 700px
  - `FoundryPanel.svelte` — scene name, combat round/turn status strip
  - `SessionControls.svelte` — Start/Stop toggle, session timer, Share Link with clipboard feedback
- `App.svelte` rewritten to compose all 5 panels, own IPC subscriptions, manage speaker color map, handle delta merges, serve mock transcript (10 segments + one every 12s)
- Fixes required post-delivery: Svelte 4 `new App()` call left alongside Svelte 5 `mount()` — removed; `vite.config.mjs` needed `base: './'` for Electron asset path resolution

### Step 4 — Integration Testing
- Built `src/pipeline/stability_test.py` — v0.1 integration stability harness adapted from
  `poc/phase3/stability_test.py`. Connects to the running pipeline via WebSocket (not inline
  like Phase 3), simulates 3 concurrent audio streams, polls VRAM every 30s via pynvml,
  writes benchmark results to `projects/Lumen/benchmarks/v01_integration.md`
- Built `src/hub/main/pipeline-client.js` — WebSocket client in Electron main process that
  connects to the pipeline on `ws://127.0.0.1:8090`, receives transcript segments, and relays
  them to the renderer via IPC
- `preload.js` extended with 3 new IPC bridge methods: onPipelineConnected,
  onPipelineDisconnected, onTranscriptSegment, getPipelineStatus
- `App.svelte` updated to subscribe to pipeline IPC events. On first real segment: clears mock
  data, stops mock feed, switches to live transcript. On pipeline disconnect: mock feed resumes.
  Pipeline status shown in StatusBar.

## Validated Live

Full end-to-end test with Foundry VTT, pipeline, and Hub all running simultaneously:
- Foundry module connected, HELLO acknowledged, game state streaming
- Pipeline connected, transcript segments flowing into TranscriptPanel
- Real speaker-attributed transcript visible in Hub UI (Tariel, Linn, Ikyulys)
- Foundry state panel showing scene "Arch Plaza", party data correct
- 30 minutes continuous operation, 0 errors

## 30-Minute Stability Test Results

| Metric | Phase 3 Baseline | v0.1 Result | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| Duration | 60 min | 30 min | — |
| Streams | 3 | 3 | — |
| Segments transcribed | 16,548 | 8,949 | on pace |
| Errors | 0 | 0 | ✅ |
| VRAM avg | 6,349 MB | ~6,534 MB | +2.9% |
| VRAM peak | 6,393 MB | 6,625 MB | +3.6% |
| VRAM leak | None | None | ✅ |
| Regression threshold | — | 6,984 MB | ✅ passed |

Latency improved vs Phase 3 (validated in 2-min pre-run):
- Latency avg: 1,410ms (-16% from Phase 3 baseline of 1,681ms)
- Latency p95: 3,474ms (-13% from Phase 3 baseline of 3,996ms)

VRAM higher than Phase 3 because Electron Hub now running alongside pipeline —
expected, accounted for, well within threshold.

ALL REGRESSION CHECKS PASSED.

Benchmark outputs written to:
- `projects/Lumen/benchmarks/v01_integration.md`
- `projects/Lumen/benchmarks/v01_transcript.jsonl`
- `projects/Lumen/benchmarks/v01_transcript.txt`

## Facts Learned
- Foundry v13 converted client classes to ES modules (previously global scope)
- `foundry-vtt-types` for v13 is still in beta with known bugs
- PF2e stores conditions as items of type "condition" on actors, accessible via `actor.itemTypes.condition`
- Actor ownership level 3 = OWNER permission in Foundry
- Pipeline WebSocket server runs on port 8090 (not 17173 as originally specced)
- Pipeline client backoff at startup is correct behavior when stability test starts before Hub is fully up

## Known Issues / Notes
- Session timer in Hub UI reset mid-test — caused by Vite hot-reload during dev run, not a real bug. Will not occur in packaged app.
- Auto-scroll "New messages below" indicator triggers under synthetic 3-stream load — not representative of real session load, not a bug.
- Players show `active: false` — expected, no real VoIP connections yet.
- Share Link is a placeholder (`http://127.0.0.1:17171/join`) — real link in Step 4.
- `lumen.md` Key Facts needs port updated from 17173 to 8090.

## v0.1 Complete

All four steps done and validated:
- Step 1: Project restructure and pipeline validation ✅
- Step 2: Foundry VTT v13 module ✅
- Step 3: Hub transcript viewer UI (Electron + Svelte) ✅
- Step 4: Integration testing — 30 min, 0 errors, no regressions ✅

Lumen v0.1 ships with: real-time transcription, speaker attribution, Foundry game
state streaming (scene/combat/party), live Hub UI, and a validated stable pipeline
on RTX 3070 (8GB VRAM) with large-v3.

## Up Next
Decision needed: v0.2 (suggestion engine) or park Lumen and return to Sylvara
(current priority #1). Sylvara revenue funds Lumen long-term.

## Keywords
lumen, step-0, step-1, step-2, step-3, step-4, integration, stability-test, v0.1-complete, pipeline, vram, foundry-module, websocket, pairing, svelte, electron
